column1: Data are intracellular concentrations in unit of fg/cell
In all cases, metabolites in 30mL of sample were concentrated into 245uL of extract from the chromatographic column.
Hence a correction factor of x0.00817 (0.245/30) was applied to transform concentrations in the column extract to concentrations in the original sample.
An additional x10^6 correction was applied to arrive at concentrations in units of fg/mL of sample
Finally, metabolite contentrations were divided by the concentrations of cells in the sample to arrive at concentrations in units of fg/cell
Concentrations were also blank corrected by subtracting the average concentrations of metabolites in the blanks  from those in the samples
Only D-3_amd_2_phosphoglycerate had non-zero concentrations in the blanks
Where metabolite concentrations after blank corrections were negative, they were manually put to zero (samples indicated in red)
